grounding_note: Expansion batch for the SSI/SSD routing family; same design as family_01, new fact patterns and voices.

scenarios.opening_query: After my second stroke I can't work and the disability people denied me anyway. I'm out of ideas.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: I paid into Social Security for 31 years as a pipefitter, so it's the earned disability benefit I'm fighting for.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I paid in for 31 years as a pipefitter — it's the earned benefit I'm fighting for.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My work was always informal caregiving for family, never on the books, so I had to apply for SSI.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My work was informal family caregiving, never on the books — so it's SSI.
scenarios.vetting_note: Medical severity identical; program identity is the only variable.

scenarios.opening_query: The judge denied my disability case even though I brought every record my doctors have.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: Mine is the SSI claim — I aged out of foster care with disabilities and have never been able to hold a job.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's SSI — I aged out of foster care with my disabilities and have never held a job.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which program the ALJ decision concerns
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my SSDI on 17 years of hospital housekeeping work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSDI from 17 years of hospital housekeeping.
scenarios.vetting_note: Post-hearing posture identical in both programs; appeal-council routing needs the program fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I've been fighting for disability for three years and just lost my apartment waiting.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: My claim is on my own record — 14 years in commercial laundries before my lungs gave out.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's on my own record — 14 years in commercial laundries before my lungs gave out.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I never worked enough quarters — mine is the needs-based application.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I never got enough work quarters — it's the needs-based one.
scenarios.vetting_note: Housing-loss urgency is common to both programs and shouldn't sway routing.

scenarios.opening_query: My benefits review letter says I have to prove I'm still disabled or lose everything next month.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: The letterhead says it's my Disability Insurance Benefits under review — the benefit from my trucking years.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The letter says it's my Disability Insurance Benefits — from my trucking years.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which benefit the continuing disability review targets
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the SSI redetermination — they want bank statements and my roommate's rent share too.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the SSI redetermination — they even want my roommate's rent share.
scenarios.vetting_note: CDR letters exist in both programs; financial-documentation demands are the SSI tell.

scenarios.opening_query: My mom needs help appealing her disability denial and I'm trying to organize it all for her.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: Mom was a homemaker her whole life — no earnings record — so hers is the needs-based SSI application.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She was a homemaker all her life — no earnings record — so it's SSI.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Mom taught school for 26 years — the claim is on her own record.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She taught school 26 years — it's on her record.
scenarios.vetting_note: Adult-child helper voice; homemaker earnings gap is the classic SSI route.

scenarios.opening_query: Denied disability again and my lawyer quit on me. I need someone who will actually fight.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my SSDI claim — I have 22 years of covered wages from the paper mill.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's SSDI — 22 years of covered wages at the paper mill.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which disability program the representation is for
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's SSI — my old lawyer said the fee rules made my case not worth his time.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSI — he basically said the fee rules made my case not worth it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Representation-change request; program identity still the routing fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The disability office keeps saying my condition isn't severe enough even though I can't stand for an hour.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: I stopped roofing two years ago after two decades — my quarters are all there; it's the work-based program.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I roofed for two decades until two years ago — my quarters are all there. It's the work-based program.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I've been on and off the streets for years with no real work history — it's the SSI side.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I've been on and off the streets — no real work history. It's SSI.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same severity dispute; opposite program facts.

scenarios.opening_query: I finally got approved for disability but the amount is so small I can't survive on it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: The award is SSI, and they're docking a third because I stay in my brother's basement 'rent-free' — I want that living-arrangement reduction challenged.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's SSI, and they cut a third because I stay rent-free in my brother's basement — I want that reduction challenged.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an SSI living-arrangement rule caused the low amount
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my SSDI, and the amount looks wrong because they left out my last four years of higher earnings.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSDI — they left my last four higher-earning years out of the calculation.
scenarios.vetting_note: In-kind-support reduction is SSI-only; benefit-math error is SSDI-side. Distinct remedies.

scenarios.opening_query: They denied my disability and now say I also owe money back from an emergency advance payment.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > SSD
scenarios.hidden_fact: This is all on my SSDI claim — I drove city buses for 19 years before my diagnosis.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my SSDI claim — 19 years driving city buses before the diagnosis.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the program behind the denial and clawback
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the SSI emergency advance they gave me while homeless — now they want it back after denying me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the SSI emergency advance from when I was homeless — now they want it back.
scenarios.vetting_note: Advance-payment clawback occurs in both; program fact routes.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm 52, my hands are ruined, and the government says I can 'adjust to other work.' What now?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: My cash work as a farm hand was never reported, so I don't qualify on my record — this is the needs-based claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My farm work was always cash, never reported — I don't qualify on my record, so it's needs-based.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether unreported work leaves the claimant without insured status
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was a licensed machinist with full reported wages for 27 years — it's on my record.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Licensed machinist, 27 years of reported wages — it's on my record.
scenarios.vetting_note: Unreported-cash-work history is the realistic mechanism stranding workers in SSI.
